title: nasa rugosa subsp. rugosa
intro: Nasa rugosa subsp. rugosa, also known as Nasa or Chilean tea, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Nasa rugosa subsp. rugosa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties, with the leaves and flowers being the primary components used in traditional medicine.
